paper_title: Materials and Methods of Tamiami Formation Study

methods.description: Collection of marine shell-rich samples from the Tamiami Formation for further analysis.

steps.description: Material used for this study was collected in July 2017 by two of us (EDM and RWP) from Phase 10 excavations (now water-filled) of the SMR Aggregates quarries, situated northeast of Sarasota, Florida.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples from SMR Aggregates quarries

steps.description: We picked 867 shells (left valves) of the bivalve Anomia simplex d 'Orbigny, 1853, commonly known as 'jingle shells', visibly encrusted by bryozoans, from Units 10/11.
steps.searchable_summary: pick 867 Anomia simplex shells from Units 10/11

methods.description: Identification and classification of collected bryozoan species based on skeletal characters.

steps.description: Oncousoecia sp. is the only cyclostome species present in our sample. Species-level identification is difficult on the basis of a single colony, given the paucity and high variability of skeletal characters among cyclostomes.
steps.searchable_summary: identify Oncousoecia sp. based on skeletal characters
